title: In vitro testing of guide RNA efficiency for CRISPR-mediated genome editing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"424js","text":"In CRISPR-mediated genome editing, guide RNAs (gRNAs) serve to target the Cas9 nuclease to an intended target genomic locus. Many gRNAs are not efficient in facilitating Cas9-mediated cleavage by introduction of double-strand breaks. Therefore, after designing possible gRNA sequences via web-based tools, it is critical to assess their cleavage efficiency at the target locus and identify the most efficient gRNA(s) in vitro before deploying in binary plasmids for genome editing in planta.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":417,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":481,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

70 pmol","mol_weight":0,"sku":"M0386S","url":"https://www.neb.com/M0386","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":2,"name":"New England Biolabs","link":"https://www.neb.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/2.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/2.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Perform DNA extraction using CTAB method or any other methods that you may use routinely for your plant species.
Design primers flanking your target PAM sites (NGG). PCR to amplify around 500-600 bp of genomic/gene fragment around the target. Large size templates are easy to resolve on gel electrophoresis.

Clean PCR product using Zymo/Bioneer DNA cleanup kit and Sanger sequence to confirm that your PCR amplifications are intended gene. This will also help later for screening indel polymorphisms at PAM site.

Design targets for editing target exon(s) using gRNA design tool ( http://crispr.mit.edu/ or any other portal). The following primers are then used to synthesize the DNA template to prepare gRNA for each target. Oligo design a. Primer 1 (specific for each guide): a) TAATACGACTCCTATAGGG-(20 bp guide sequence)-gtttAagagctaTGCTGgaa b) The three bolded G’s are included in the transcript and required for transcription. If the guide-specific sequence starts with one or more G’s, the same number of bolded G’s can be deleted from the primer. b. Primer 2 (always the same): a) Reverse-gRNA Template: aaaagcaccgactcggtgccactttttcaagttgataacggactagccttatttAaacttgctaTGCTGtttc CAGCAtagctctTaaac
Set up reaction in PCR tube: 7 µL water (nuclease free) 5 µL NTP mix 10 millimolar (mM) each 2 µL Template DNA (∼ 100 ng — 500 ng ) 1 µL T7 RNA Polymerase Mix Total 15 µL
Use HiScribe T7 High Yield RNA Synthesis Kit (NEB E2050S)
Incubate at 37 °C for 02:00:00 .
Move the reaction into 1.5 mL eppendorf tube.
Add 435 µL DNAase/RNAse free water.
Measure gRNA concentration on nanodrop. Note it down. (May not be accurate, as NTPs may inflate the nanodrop readings).
Optional to cleanup using any RNA clean-up kit. Dilute to 10 ng / μL by adding nuclease free water.

Set up the following PCR reaction: 30.5 µL nuclease free water 10 µL 5X Phusion buffer 1 µL 10 millimolar (mM) dNTP 4 µL primer 1 ( 10 micromolar (µM) ) 4 µL primer 2 ( 10 micromolar (µM) ) Always Primer 2 (Reverse-gRNA Template) 0.5 µL Phusion polymerase Total 50 µL

Nano-drop to quantify the PCR product. Run a small aliquot of the PCR product out on a gel to check the bands. The resulting band should be 90 bp (very small) and so it is necessary to run a ≥ 3 % agarose gel .
Purify the PCR product using Zymo/bioneer DNA cleanup kit.
Set up reaction (include a negative RNA control): 7.5 µL water (nuclease free) 1.5 µL 10X Cas9 reaction buffer 1.5 µL 300 nanomolar (nM) sgRNA ( 10 ng / μL ) 0.5 µL Cas9 Nuclease Total 11 µL
Pre-incubate for 00:10:00 at 37 °C .
Add 4 µL substrate DNA (∼ 50 ng — 100 ng total).
Incubate 01:00:00 at 37 °C (or longer up to 01:30:00 ).
Denature Cas9 by incubation at 80 °C for 00:10:00 .
Set up a control reaction including all the above ingredients without gRNA. In addition, different ratios of gRNA to Cas9 nuclease can also be setup to replicate the efficiency tests.
Prepare 1.1 % volume agarose gel.
Mix the Cas9 incubated reaction with 2 µL of loading dye. Load the mixture on gel. If stain was not added during gel preparation, soak gel in staining buffer.
Run gel electrophoresis until the ladder resolves and check the cleavage of template DNA under UV illumination.
creator: Benjamin Blackman
